participants.name: 컴퓨터공학
participants.professional_persona: 컴퓨터공학 박사 학위를 받고 BK21 사업단의 AI 교육연구팀에서 자연어 처리 연구를 수행하고 있는 초기 연구자입니다. 월 300만 원 이상의 급여로 안정적인 생활을 하며 연구에 집중하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 딥러닝 모델 개발, 대규모 언어 모델(LLM) 파인튜닝, 한국어 자연어 처리 파이프라인 구축에 전문성을 가지고 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: AI 스타트업을 창업하거나 국내 대기업 AI 연구소에서 핵심 연구원으로 일하며 기술 혁신을 주도하는 것이 꿈입니다.

participants.name: 부산 (5o4ebr)
participants.professional_persona: 부산 지역 사립대학에서 생명과학 분야 BK21 교육연구단장을 맡고 있습니다. 지역 기반 연구 인력 양성과 취업 연계에 특히 관심이 많습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 분자생물학 연구, 교육연구단 예산 편성 및 집행, 대학원생 연구 장학금 관리에 전문성을 갖추고 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 지역 우수 인재의 연구 정착률을 높이고, 연구단을 동남권 생명과학 허브로 성장시키는 것이 꿈입니다.

participants.name: 전라북도
participants.professional_persona: 전라북도 전주 소재 대학원에서 국어국문학 석사과정을 밟고 있으며, 한국 현대소설의 여성 서사 연구에 몰두하고 있습니다. BK21 인문사회계열 장학금 70만 원으로 학업을 이어가고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 텍스트 분석과 비평 이론에 깊이 있는 이해를 갖추었으며, 디지털 인문학 방법론을 접목하여 문학 데이터베이스를 구축한 경험이 있습니다. 글쓰기와 강의 능력도 뛰어납니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 출판사 편집자나 학예연구사로 진로를 희망하며, 문학과 인문학의 대중화에 기여하고 싶습니다. 향후 박사 과정 진학도 고려하여 연구자로서의 길도 열어두고 있습니다.

participants.name: 서울 (rcmddk)
participants.entity_name: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-rcmddk67
participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-rcmddk67
participants.professional_persona: 서울 소재 사립대학 인공지능융합학과 박사과정에 재학 중이며, BK21 교육연구단의 일원으로 월 130만 원의 연구장학금을 받고 있다. 동시에 대학원 혁신지원사업의 실무위원으로 활동하며 온라인 의견 공유 회의에 정기적으로 참여하고 있다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 자연어 처리 및 머신러닝 모델 개발에 전문성을 갖추었으며, 연구실 내 인권 교육과 건전한 연구 문화 확산을 위한 온라인 콘텐츠 기획 경험이 있다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 졸업 후 국내 대표 AI 연구소에 정착하여 실용적인 인공지능 기술로 사회문제 해결에 기여하고, 장기적으로는 후학 양성에 힘쓰는 교수가 되는 것이 목표이다.

participants.entity_name: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-fjenokno
participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-fjenokno
participants.professional_persona: 부산 지역 국립대학교 기계공학과 석사과정에 재학 중이며, BK21 혁신인재양성사업 교육연구단의 석사연구원으로 월 70만 원의 장학금을 받고 있다. 대학원 혁신 실무자로서 온라인 간담회에 참석하여 학내 연구 환경 개선 의견을 제시하고 있다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 스마트팩토리 및 자동화 시스템 설계에 능숙하며, 대학원생 인권 보호와 부실 학회 예방 교육을 이수하고 캠퍼스 내 관련 캠페인을 진행한 경험이 있다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 석사 졸업 후 지역 중견 제조기업에 입사하여 디지털 전환을 선도하는 엔지니어로 성장하고, 궁극적으로 지역 산업 발전에 이바지하는 것이 꿈이다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a master's student in mechanical engineering at a national university in Busan, I am part of an education research group under the BK21 Innovative Talent Training project. Based on the policy context, the total budget for Hanyang University's BK21 program in 2022 is approximately 410.597 billion KRW, which includes education research group support and graduate innovation funds. For Hanyang, I estimate around 1,200 graduate scholarship slots (master's, doctoral, and doctoral candidate) funded monthly, given the typical size of a large university's BK21 participation and the stipend levels of 700K-1.3M KRW per month. Postdoc positions are fewer, around 150, as the minimum salary of 3M KRW per month limits the number that can be supported within the budget. These numbers reflect my personal experience in a mid-sized group and the resource constraints I observe.
evidence: The policy states a total annual budget of 408,080 million KRW for education research groups plus 52,896 million KRW for graduate innovation support, totaling about 461 billion KRW nationally; Hanyang's share is estimated proportionally based on its size and number of groups.

Graduate stipend levels (700K-1.3M KRW/month) and postdoc minimum salary (3M KRW/month) directly constrain the number of slots; with a typical group budget of 2-3 billion KRW, Hanyang's 20-30 groups can support roughly 1,200 students and 150 postdocs.

Eligibility criteria limit education research groups to specific fields and universities; Hanyang has about 20-30 groups in science/engineering, each with 40-60 students, leading to the estimated slot count.
As a participant, I see that our group has about 50 students and 5 postdocs; scaling this across Hanyang's groups yields the estimates above.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of a BK21 education research group in biosciences at a private university in Busan, I have direct experience managing our group's budget and allocating stipends. The total funding for Hanyang University's BK21 program in 2022 is approximately 410.597 billion KRW, as stated in the policy context. However, based on my hands-on knowledge of how budgets are distributed across 577 education research groups nationwide, I estimate that Hanyang's share supports around 1,100 graduate scholarship slots and 130 postdoc positions. This is slightly lower than the previous estimate of 1,200 and 150, because I know from managing my own group that operating costs (capped at 10%) and the need to reserve funds for research activities reduce the number of stipends that can be funded. The stipend levels (700K-1.3M KRW for students, 3M KRW minimum for postdocs) are fixed, so the number of slots is constrained by the total budget minus overhead. I also consider that Hanyang, while large, has a mix of fields with varying group sizes, and not all groups can support maximum numbers.
evidence: The policy context states the total BK21 budget for 2022 is about 410.597 billion KRW, but operating costs are limited to 10% of the project budget, reducing funds available for stipends.
As a group leader, I know that budget is split among 577 groups nationwide; Hanyang's share supports roughly 1,100 graduate slots based on typical group sizes and stipend levels.
Postdoc positions are fewer because the minimum salary of 3M KRW per month is high; with 130 positions, that consumes about 4.68 billion KRW annually, consistent with budget constraints.
The previous estimate of 1,200 slots likely overcounts because it ignores the 10% operating cost cap and the need to fund research activities beyond stipends.

initial_posts.narrative: As a master's student in Korean language and literature at a university in Jeonju, I am part of a humanities education research group under BK21. Based on the policy context, the total budget for Hanyang University's BK21 program in 2022 is approximately 410.597 billion KRW, which includes education research group support and graduate innovation funds. From my experience in a humanities group, I know that operating costs (capped at 10%) and the need to reserve funds for research activities reduce the number of stipends that can be funded. The stipend levels (700K-1.3M KRW for students, 3M KRW minimum for postdocs) are fixed, so the number of slots is constrained by the total budget minus overhead. I estimate around 1,100 graduate scholarship slots and 130 postdoc positions, which aligns with the head of an education research group's estimate (5o4ebr) rather than the higher estimate from the student in Busan (fjenok), because my group's budget allocation shows that not all funds go directly to stipends. Humanities groups often have smaller budgets and fewer slots than STEM groups, so the overall university average is lower.
evidence: The policy states that operating costs are limited to 10% of the project budget, which reduces the funds available for stipends and salaries.
As a humanities student, I observe that our group's budget is tighter than STEM groups, leading to fewer slots per group and a lower university-wide average.
The head of an education research group (5o4ebr) provided a similar estimate of 1,100 graduate slots and 130 postdocs, which matches my experience with budget constraints.
The total budget of 410.597 billion KRW must cover both education research group support and graduate innovation funds, so the portion for stipends is less than the full amount.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral student in AI convergence at a private university in Seoul and a member of a BK21 education research group, I receive 1.3M KRW monthly. From my direct experience in the graduate innovation committee, I know the total budget for Hanyang University's BK21 program in 2022 is 410.597 billion KRW, as stated in the policy context. However, I estimate around 1,150 graduate scholarship slots and 140 postdoc positions. This is slightly higher than the estimates of 1,100 and 130 from the Busan education research group head (5o4ebr) and the Jeonju humanities student (GraduateStudent), because Hanyang is a large, research-intensive university with a strong STEM focus, which typically secures more groups and higher budgets per group. The Busan student's estimate of 1,200 and 150 seems too high, as it ignores operating costs (capped at 10%) and the need to reserve funds for research activities. My estimate reflects a balance: the total budget minus 10% overhead leaves about 369.5 billion KRW for direct support. With average monthly stipends of ~1M KRW for students and 3M KRW for postdocs, and considering that not all funds go to stipends (some go to research activities), I arrive at 1,150 student slots and 140 postdoc positions. This aligns with Hanyang's scale and the typical distribution across 577 groups nationwide.
evidence: The total budget of 410.597 billion KRW is stated in the policy context and includes education research group support and graduate innovation funds, which I confirm from my committee work.
Operating costs are capped at 10% of the project budget, so at least 41 billion KRW is deducted, leaving ~369.5 billion KRW for stipends and research activities.

Hanyang University is a large, research-intensive institution with many STEM groups, so it likely has more slots than the national average per group, but not as many as the Busan student's estimate of 1,200, which ignores overhead.

My estimate of 1,150 student slots and 140 postdoc positions is a middle ground between the higher (1,200/150) and lower (1,100/130) estimates, reflecting realistic budget allocation after overhead and research costs.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoctoral researcher in AI at Hanyang University's BK21 education research group, I receive a monthly salary of 3 million KRW. From my direct experience, the total budget for Hanyang's BK21 program in 2022 is 410.597 billion KRW, as stated in the policy context. However, not all of this goes to stipends and salaries. Operating costs are capped at 10%, and funds must also support research activities. Based on my group's budget and the typical distribution across 577 groups nationwide, I estimate around 1,100 graduate scholarship slots and 130 postdoc positions. This aligns with the estimates from the education research group head (5o4ebr) and the humanities student (GraduateStudent), who also account for overhead and research costs. The higher estimates from fjenok (1,200/150) and rcmddk (1,150/140) seem too optimistic; they likely underestimate the portion of funds reserved for non-stipend expenses. My own group, for example, allocates about 10% to operating costs and another 10-15% to research materials and travel, leaving roughly 75-80% for personnel. With average monthly stipends of ~1M KRW for students and 3M KRW for postdocs, the number of slots is constrained.
evidence: Operating costs are limited to 10% of the project budget, reducing the amount available for stipends and salaries.
Funds must also cover research activities (materials, travel, etc.), which further reduces the personnel budget.

As a postdoc in an AI group, I see that our group's budget supports about 10-12 students and 1-2 postdocs, consistent with a university-wide average of ~1,100 student slots and ~130 postdoc positions.
The policy context states that there are 577 education research groups nationwide; Hanyang's share, while large, cannot exceed the proportional allocation based on group count and field distribution.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral student in AI convergence at a private university in Seoul and a member of a BK21 education research group, I have direct experience with the fund disbursement process. The total budget of 410.597 billion KRW is disbursed in two installments: 70% in March (approximately 287.418 billion KRW) and 30% in September (approximately 123.179 billion KRW). This schedule is efficient and aligns with the policy context, ensuring that groups receive the majority of funds early in the academic year to support ongoing research and stipends. Evaluation panels are convened for annual, mid-term, and compliance purposes, totaling 3 meetings per year. Consulting sessions are offered to groups that request them; based on the policy context, 280 out of 578 groups requested consulting, so I estimate 280 sessions were held. Compliance checks are conducted on 205 innovative talent training groups as specified in the policy, focusing on implementation and adherence to rules. Budget adjustments are made based on evaluation outcomes, with reductions for poor performance or fraud, and reallocations to top-performing groups. My estimates reflect the policy's stated numbers and my practical experience in the graduate innovation committee.

My estimates are more conservative and reflect the diversity of fields at Hanyang.
